description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, blocky stone with a mix of smooth and jagged surfaces, featuring a dominant gray coloration with streaks of white and rusty brown. The stone has a visibly fractured texture, with some faces appearing freshly broken and others showing signs of natural weathering. One side is marked with black alphanumeric text, likely for identification or cataloging purposes. The top surface is relatively flat with a dark vein running across it, while the sides display a combination of angular cuts and rough, uneven patches. The stone's coloration transitions from pale gray to rusty orange, suggesting mineral inclusions or exposure to the elements. Its overall appearance is that of a quarried or naturally split stone, suitable for use as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description: A blocky, gray stone with rough and smooth surfaces, streaked with rusty brown and white, and marked with black text. It features fractured edges and a flat top with a dark vein.
description_view_0: The front face is flat and gray, prominently displaying black alphanumeric text and subtle veining.
description_view_1: This view highlights the rough, rusty-brown side with more pronounced weathering and irregular texture.
description_view_2: The side shows a mix of gray and brown hues, with a visible dark vein and some surface mottling.
description_view_3: The back face is mostly pale gray and smoother, with less visible texturing and a sharp edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a barrier along pathways
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and mottling with a dark linear streak on the top surface; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Partially weathered with some rough, eroded surfaces and areas that appear freshly broken.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban environments, but can blend with natural landscaping or other stone features.

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
